// description: Opt-in Claude Code hooks that make the praxis memory ledger automatic. Copy the `hooks` block into a CONSUMING repo's .claude/settings.json (merge if it already has hooks). Requires the `memory` skill present at .claude/skills/memory/ (install the praxis plugin, or `make export SKILL=memory TO=<repo>`). praxis ships this as a template and does not enable it on itself.
// SessionStart: Ensure the ledger exists, then orient the session: `bootstrap --brief` prints one line — a suggestion to run `/memory init` when the ledger is empty (the closest thing to an on-install seed), or a count of what's on record otherwise — and `pending --brief` surfaces entries awaiting accept/reject, so nothing is forgotten between sessions.
// Stop: When a turn ends with uncommitted changes, capture a de-duplicated, rollback-able snapshot automatically. Combined with the doctrine in the memory skill (which records the WHY of decisions), this guarantees a durable record across every command and skill.
// PreToolUse.git-commit-push: Before a commit or push lands — the moment work is about to ship — re-surface the ledger entries still pending accept/reject as a stderr nudge, so work authorized only by a pending decision or plan is caught before it ships (pending is not approval; stop condition U-11). A reminder, not a gate: exit 0 always, never blocks — planning runs legitimately commit with everything pending.

hooks.command: python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/skills/memory/scripts/ledger.py" init >/dev/null 2>&1; python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/skills/memory/scripts/ledger.py" bootstrap --brief 2>/dev/null; python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/skills/memory/scripts/ledger.py" pending --brief 2>/dev/null; exit 0
python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/skills/memory/scripts/ledger.py" snapshot --source auto 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/💾 praxis memory: captured snapshot /;t;d' >&2; exit 0
